Dutch Mob.description: The Dutch Mob was a New York pickpocket gang formed in the late 1860s by Little Freddie, Mike Kurtz, and Johnny Irving. It operated in Manhattan's Bowery area and became one of the largest pickpocket gangs in the United States with around 300 members. The gang used tactics such as staging street fights to pickpocket crowds. It was largely dismantled by police crackdowns in 1877.

Nueva Plaza Cartel.description: Mexican criminal organization based in Jalisco, formed in 2017 as a split from the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG). Active in the Guadalajara metropolitan area and states of Jalisco, Colima, and Guanajuato, with reported financing from the Sinaloa Cartel. Engaged in drug trafficking, homicide, torture, fuel theft, and home invasion.

Gran Grif.description: Gran Grif is the largest and most powerful criminal organization in the Artibonite department of Haiti, based in Savien. Founded around 2016 by former congressman Prophane Victor, it engages in massacres, kidnappings, sexual violence, and forced recruitment of children. The group was responsible for 80% of reported civilian deaths in Artibonite in 2022 and has been designated a terrorist organization by the Dominican Republic and the United States.

Hammerskins.description: Neo-Nazi group formed in 1988 in Dallas, Texas, focused on producing and promoting white power rock music. It is one of the most prominent American white power skinhead groups, with chapters in the US, Canada, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand. The group is self-described as 'leaderless' and has been involved in violent attacks and hate crimes, though not centrally organized. Its website is defunct and online forums inactive as of 2020.

Sicilian Mafia.description: Cosa Nostra is a Sicilian mafia-type criminal organization based in Italy, primarily in Sicily, with international branches. It originated in the 19th century and became known for its violent activities and influence. The Italian state intensified countermeasures in the 1980s, leading to the deaths of prominent anti-mafia magistrates. It remains active today.

edges.evidence_quote: Il rapporto con la 'Ndrangheta è stato molto stretto tanto che capibastone di spicco come Antonio Macrì, Giuseppe Piromalli, Mico Tripodo (compare d'anello di Totò Riina) si affiliarono a Cosa nostra e viceversa, capi della mafia siciliana si affiliano alle 'ndrine.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta participated in Cosa Nostra's bombing campaign in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2017 l'operazione 'Ndrangheta stragista rivelerebbe un primo coinvolgimento della 'ndrangheta nelle operazioni stragiste degli anni '90 di Cosa nostra.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra buys drugs from 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal XXI secolo si registra un declino dell'organizzazione siciliana rispetto a quella di origine campana e calabrese, il procuratore di Catanzaro Nicola Gratteri ha più volte affermato questa tendenza, tanto da dichiarare, supportato dalle numerose operazioni anti-ndrangheta, che ora Cosa nostra messinese si rifornisce dalle 'ndrine per l'acquisto di droga

descriptions: Cosa Nostra and 'Ndrangheta cooperate in drug trafficking and waste management.
edges.evidence_quote: A novembre 2017 il neoprocuratore nazionale antimafia Federico Cafiero De Raho conferma il rapporto tra le due organizzazioni criminali non solo nel campo degli stupefacenti, ma anche nell'ambito del settore dei rifiuti e della bonifica

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta members provided support to Cosa Nostra boss Matteo Messina Denaro while he was a fugitive.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 23 marzo 2018 l'Espresso rivela che il latitante Matteo Messina Denaro avrebbe avuto appoggi da esponenti della 'ndrangheta in Toscana

descriptions: Cosa Nostra regent Nunzio Serio relied on 'Ndrangheta member Emanuele Cosentino to import drugs from Calabria.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2025, nell'ambito dell'operazione del Comando provinciale dei Carabinieri di Palermo ... è emerso che Nunzio Serio, reggente del mandamento di Tommaso Natale, faceva riferimento ad Emanuele Cosentino, esponente della 'ndrina Gallico di Palmi, per importare grandi quantitativi di cocaina e hashish dalla Calabria in Sicilia.

descriptions: The text groups Sicilian Mafia, 'Ndrangheta, and Camorra as major Italian criminal organizations structured around families.
edges.evidence_quote: In addition to the traditional families of the Sicilian Mafia, other major Italian criminal organizations—particularly the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria and the Camorra in Campania, are also structured around criminal families.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra yielded the drug trade to 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: By the late 1990s, the weakened Cosa Nostra had to yield most of the illegal drug trade to the 'Ndrangheta crime organization from Calabria.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta surpassed Cosa Nostra as a more reliable partner for international drug trafficking organizations.
edges.evidence_quote: Cosa nostra aveva ormai perso la leadership nel traffico mondiale di droga a vantaggio della 'ndrangheta calabrese , ritenuta un partner più affidabile dalle organizzazioni internazionali del narcotraffico.

descriptions: The Arena 'Ndrangheta cosca had direct contact with Cosa Nostra bosses Pietro Vernengo and Nitto Santapaola.
edges.evidence_quote: Le indagini dei Carabinieri hanno rivelato che Nicola Arena, a capo dell'omonima cosca, era in contatto diretto con Pietro Vernengo e Nitto Santapaola, boss della mafia siciliana.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra clans from Trapani and Mazara del Vallo allied with 'Ndrangheta clans Marando and Barbaro for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2007 l'operazione Stupor Mundi sgomina un traffico internazionale di droga gestito dai Marando e dai Barbaro di Platì che portavano la droga passante dal Belgio e dai Paesi Bassi in Piemonte e Lombardia in alleanza con le cosche del trapanese, di Mazara del Vallo.

descriptions: Condello 'ndrina participated in a meeting with Cosa Nostra representatives.
edges.evidence_quote: come rappresentante delle prime (in particolare i Tegano, Condello, Latella, Ficara, Serraino e Imerti) partecipò Giuseppe De Stefano e per i secondi Giuseppe e Filippo Graviano

descriptions: Cosa Nostra clans (Agate, Guttadauro) allied with 'Ndrangheta clans (Marando, Trimboli) for international drug trafficking.

descriptions: Sicilian Mafia and American Mafia met to discuss heroin trade.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 1957, a meeting between Sicilian Mafia and American Mafia members was held at the Grand Hotel et des Palmes in Palermo to discuss the international illegal heroin trade in the French Connection.

descriptions: Italian crime groups collaborate with Italian-American Mafia, which is an offshoot of Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: These Italian crime groups frequently collaborate with the Italian-American Mafia, which is itself an offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia.

descriptions: Both organizations were defendants in the same trial for a joint heroin trafficking operation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Pizza Connection Trial (in full, United States v. Badalamenti et al. ) was a criminal trial against the Sicilian Mafia and American Mafia

descriptions: The Sicilian Mafia and Italian-American Mafia are related organizations sharing the name Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: the Sicilian Mafia and the related Italian-American mafia refer to their organizations as "Cosa nostra"

descriptions: The Sicilian Mafia and its American counterpart are studied together as related organized crime groups.
edges.evidence_quote: Academic studies of the original "Mafia", the Sicilian Mafia , as well as its American counterpart , generated an economic study of organized crime groups and exerted great influence on studies of the Russian bratva , the Indonesian preman , the Chinese triads , the Hong Kong triads, the Indian thuggee , and the Japanese yakuza .

descriptions: The American Mafia is an offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia, created by Sicilian emigrants.
edges.evidence_quote: By the 20th century, wide-scale emigration from Sicily led to the formation of mafiosi-style gangs in other countries, in particular in the United States, where its offshoot, the American Mafia, was created.

descriptions: The American Mafia is an offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia, formed by Sicilian emigrants.

edges.evidence_quote: l'iniziativa della banda venne quindi bloccata dalla fazione dei Corleonesi contraria alla liberazione che, attraverso il suo referente romano Giuseppe Calò, intervenne

descriptions: Black Axe allied with the Porta Nuova clan of Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: Si scopre quindi un'alleanza tra il clan palermitano e l'organizzazione nigeriana.

descriptions: Mano nera was founded by mafiosi emigrating from Sicily, acting as a sister organization.
edges.evidence_quote: alle migliaia di emigranti siciliani che lasciarono l'isola si mescolarono spesso delinquenti mafiosi, che andarono a fondare un' organizzazione sorella d'oltreoceano specializzata nel taglieggiamento dei suoi connazionali (definita " Mano nera "

descriptions: Cosa Nostra had contacts with the C.A.M.E.A. masonic lodge, collaborating in the simulated kidnapping of Michele Sindona.
edges.evidence_quote: Secondo Buscetta, il primo contatto fra Cosa nostra e massoneria avvenne nel contesto del tentativo golpista di Junio Valerio Borghese dell'8 dicembre 1970 e sarebbe proseguito nell'estate-autunno 1979 con l'aiuto fornito al banchiere Michele Sindona (affiliato alla P2 di Gelli) da parte della loggia C.A.M.E.A. insieme al gruppo Bontate - Spatola - Inzerillo, che simularono il suo sequestro, forse a fini di ricatto per salvare il denaro bruciato nella bancarotta sindoniana oppure per preparare la secessione della Sicilia dal resto d'Italia in funzione anti-comunista.

descriptions: Camorra members were admitted into Cosa Nostra and formed branches in Campania.

Come "premio", i camorristi campani furono ammessi in Cosa nostra e formarono delle succursali in Campania

descriptions: Camorra clans have had relationships with Cosa Nostra, mainly in cigarette smuggling and drug trafficking.

descriptions: Camorra collaborated with Cosa Nostra and other groups under Russian mafia supervision.

descriptions: The text groups Sicilian Mafia, 'Ndrangheta, and Camorra as major Italian criminal organizations structured around families.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra's Corleonesi faction had contacts with the Lubrano-Ligato Camorra clan.

descriptions: The Corleonesi are a faction that emerged within Cosa Nostra after the Second Mafia War.

Cárteles Unidos.description: Mexican criminal organization formed in 2010 in Michoacán, originally as La Resistencia, to expel Los Zetas from Michoacán and Jalisco. Composed of members from various cartels including Sinaloa, Gulf, and La Familia Michoacana. Engages in drug trafficking, extortion, and armed attacks, primarily in the Tierra Caliente region. Designated a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. State Department in February 2025. Currently active and in conflict with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

Azusa 13.description: Azusa 13 is a street gang based in Azusa, California, founded in the 1960s. It is one of the most aggressive Sureño gangs, with around 400 active members. The gang is affiliated with the Mexican Mafia and engages in drug trafficking, extortion, and hate crimes, particularly targeting African Americans. Its activities have led to federal indictments and convictions for ethnic cleansing campaigns.
